results.note: Plan phase complete: mkdirSync static import and all three gates (build/test/lint) verified as already passing; PLAN.md and PLAN-SUMMARY.json written; task transitioned draft → planned.
Plan approved: mkdirSync already statically imported, save() has no await, CLAUDE.md has no stale entry. Ready for implementation.

All acceptance criteria verified: mkdirSync is statically imported in src/store/graph.ts, save() has no await, and all three gates (build/test/lint) exit 0. No code changes were required. PROGRESS.md and IMPLEMENTATION-SUMMARY.json written.
All 6 acceptance criteria independently verified: mkdirSync static import, no await in save(), build/test/lint all exit 0, no stale CLAUDE.md entry.
All 6 acceptance criteria passed: mkdirSync static import confirmed, save() synchronous, build/test/lint all exit 0, no stale CLAUDE.md entries.
Task CART-S01-T01 approved — mkdirSync static import verified, save() is synchronous, all gates pass.
CART-S01-T01 writeback phase complete: gate-less finalization for approved task. mkdirSync static import verified, all gates (build, test, lint) passed, validation approved.
Task CART-S01-T01 committed: mkdirSync static import verified and all gates passing (build, test, lint). Status transitioned from approved to committed.

logs: Task CART-S01-T01 (sprint CART-S01) — 8 phases: plan → review-plan → implement → review-code → validate → approve → writeback → commit
🌱 Task CART-S01-T01 complete — pipeline reached terminal (committed).
summary: Code-orchestrated port of /forge:run-task — resolve the task pipeline, drive each phase (plan→review→implement→review→validate→approve→commit) through a subagent on its ROLE_TIER model (review/validate/approve→opus, plan/implement→sonnet, commit→haiku), hold the revision loop + verdict routing + escalation in JS.

phases.title: Resolve
phases.detail: one agent reads the task manifest + config, returns the resolved pipeline phases and pre-task status

phases.title: Pipeline
phases.detail: per phase: one subagent runs the gate + phase workflow + emits its own event; JS owns the phase index, revision counters, verdict routing, and escalation decision

phases.title: Report
phases.detail: summarise the terminal outcome — committed / escalated / blocked
